conflicts.description: The prior sheet treated Chapter 15 as the point where Gavin first clearly perceives the substrate, but the manuscript already shows direct substrate perception in the Chapter 5 breakfast Levitation scene.
conflicts.canonical_value: Chapter 5 is Gavin's first direct substrate perception; Chapter 15 is the later shift to reading ordinary enchantment grammar without translation.
conflicts.resolution_note: Both beats are kept as separate stages because the Chapter 15 change is a fluency jump, not the first sighting.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet placed Gavin's first reflexive substrate editing in Chapter 17, but the manuscript shows it beginning in Chapter 16 when he starts correcting a first-year's broken wrist before consciously switching to Episkey.
conflicts.canonical_value: Chapter 16 marks the onset of reflexive First Art use; Chapter 17 shows the same ability nullifying incoming battle magic while Gavin refuses wider intervention.
conflicts.resolution_note: The Chapter 16 wrist scene is the earliest explicit reflexive edit and is harder to remove without rewriting the causality of Chapter 17.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet stretched Chapters 9-10 into January, but the manuscript places Chapter 9 in the run-up to Christmas break and Chapter 10 on Christmas Day itself.
conflicts.canonical_value: Chapters 9-10 are late December 1997, with Chapter 10 fixed to Christmas Day.
conflicts.resolution_note: The prose gives concrete holiday timing, so the seasonal track was narrowed to December rather than a December-January blend.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet said Miren did not believe Gavin's ward story until 'Chapter 17-18,' but the manuscript only gives explicit belief after the battle, in Chapter 18.
conflicts.canonical_value: Miren hears the claim in Chapter 8, sees alarming evidence during Chapter 17, and believes it in Chapter 18.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 17 provides evidence and horror, but Chapter 18 is where she explicitly accepts that the wards are real.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet summarized the manuscript as hidden in Gavin's trunk, but later chapters show him carrying pages in a folder/satchel and staging them openly on sub-collection shelves and desks.
conflicts.canonical_value: Gavin first hides the manuscript in his trunk, then routinely moves it between trunk, satchel/leather folder, and working surfaces in the sub-collection.
conflicts.resolution_note: The later mobility of the object is embedded in multiple chapters and affects both risk and workflow, so the object state was expanded rather than left static.
